THE COURT ORDERED THE FOLLOWING JUDGMENT ENTERED: PLAINTIFFS MIGUEL LOPEZ, HECTOR LOPEZ AND UNDERPINNING SPECIALIST, A PARTNERSHIP, SHALL RECOVER FROM DEFENDANT MARI KAWAGUCHI $39,130. DEFENDANT/CROSS-COMPLAINANT MARI KAWAGUCHI SHALL RECOVER $240,270 FROM PLAINTIFF/CROSS-DEFENDANTS MIGUEL LOPEZ, HECTOR LOPEZ, AND UNDERPINNING SPECIALIST. PLAINTIFF IS ENTITLED TO A SET OFF $39,130 LEAVING A BALANCE OWING $201,140 IN FAVOR OF DEFENDANT/CROSS-COMPLAINANT MARI KAWAGUCHI AND AGAINST PLAINTIFF/CROSS-DEFENDANTS MIGUEL LOPEZ, HECTOR LOPEZ AND UNDERPINNING SPECIALIST. SEE SCANNED DOCUMENT.

MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF MIGUEL LOPEZ's Motion For Order Taxing And Striking Costs.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. Miguel Enrique Lopez's motion for order taxing and striking costs is ordered off calendar. Department 302 advised Lopez on February 16, 2021 that, given Judge Stewart's retirement, Lopez should move in Department 206 for assignment to a new trial judge.

MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-03-2019 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File A Compulsory Cross-Complaint And/Or Reinstate Previously Dismissed Cross-Complaint
Off Calendar Aug-12-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Joe Bravo, Esq., Tel:415.512.6700, specially appearing for defendant and moving party;
Patrick Whitehorn, Esq. of Last and Faoro, Tel:650.696.8350, for plaintiff & opposing party, on Court Call.

RULING: The matter was not argued. There being no objection, the Court adopted the tentative ruling on DEFENDANT MARI KAWAGUCHI's Motion For Leave To File A Compulsory Cross-Complaint And/Or Reinstate Previously Dismissed Cross-Complaint, as follows:

Defendant Mari A. Kawaguchi's motion for leave to file a compulsory cross-complaint is granted. (Code Civ. Proc. 426.50, 428.50(c); Silver Organizations Ltd. v. Frank (1990) 217 Cal.App.3d 94, 98-99 [even on "eve of trial," leave to file compulsory cross-complaint is mandatory absent bad faith].) Plaintiff has not shown that Defendant is acting in bad faith or has unduly delayed to his prejudice.

Order signed in open court.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com
